steps.title: Start with the given equation and identify the goal
steps.content: We have $p = \frac{k}{4j+9}$ and need to isolate $4j+9$ to express it in terms of $p$ and $k$.

steps.title: Multiply both sides by (4j+9)
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $(4j+9)$ to move it out of the denominator:

$$p(4j+9) = k$$

steps.title: Divide both sides by p to isolate (4j+9)
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $p$:

$$4j+9 = \frac{k}{p}$$

This matches choice A — A is correct.
